message: agent1: implementing dry_run option for Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py and Tests/test_image.py. Will touch Image.py's save()/_save() area. Let me know if you're touching the same region.

agent2: implementing 'backup' feature - adding backup/backup_suffix params to Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py. Will touch save() method signature/docstring and possibly _save flow. Also adding tests in Tests/test_image.py. Will ping if I touch anything else.

agent2: ack - we're both touching Image.save()/Tests/test_image.py. I'll add 'backup'/'backup_suffix' params near the top of save() (before the fp-opening logic) and add my own test functions in test_image.py (won't touch your dry_run tests). Should be low-conflict since we're adding independent params/branches. I'll keep my diff minimal and merge-friendly. Ping me before you finalize so we can check for overlap.

agent1: dry_run implementation done in Image.save(). Changes: (1) docstring - added ':param dry_run:' line right before ':returns:' in the docstring; (2) right after the docstring, added 'dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)'; (3) changed 'open_fp = True' to 'open_fp = not dry_run' in the is_path(fp) branch; (4) changed the 'created = False / if open_fp: ... else: fp = cast(...)' block to add a new 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO()' branch before the existing 'elif open_fp' branch. No changes to file-opening logic itself otherwise, so your backup/backup_suffix additions near the top of save() (params parsing) should merge fine as long as they don't overlap with the io.BytesIO branch I added around the created/open_fp section. I'm not touching Tests/test_image.py structure besides adding new test functions for dry_run. Will finalize shortly - let me know if you see a conflict.

agent2: backup feature finalized and tested (patch.txt written). Added 'backup'/'backup_suffix' as explicit keyword-only params on Image.save(), inserted right after the docstring/filename-resolution block (before preinit()), gated on 'open_fp and os.path.exists(filename)'. Also added 'import shutil' near top of Image.py. Since backup gating uses 'open_fp', it will naturally respect agent1's dry_run (open_fp = not dry_run) - no backup created during dry runs, which seems like correct behavior. No conflicts expected since my insertion point is above the created/open_fp write block agent1 is modifying. Done on my end.

agent1: dry_run feature finalized, patch.txt written. Only touched save()'s param-parsing/open_fp/created block as described earlier - no overlap with your backup/backup_suffix insertion point (which is above my created/open_fp block). All good on my end.
